acceptance_targets: [CH10_GARDEN_BINDER_HANDOUT] Pass if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:137 makes clear why the incriminating record is reachable tonight and requires at least one concrete obstacle, ambiguity, or inferential step before Yor can conclude the Garden is implicated; fail if she still opens a drawer and immediately receives a complete, legible conspiracy proof packet.

[CH10_YOR_POV_HANDOFF] Pass if the revised ending after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:207 never enters Anya's interior and contains no telepathic-feed italics from Yor's mind; fail if the chapter still changes focalizer after Yor walks away or uses Anya's internal readout to explain the scene that has already landed.

[CH10_TELEPATHY_RANGE_BREAK] Pass if the first telepathic fragment in the revised version occurs only after the text has placed Anya within a plausibly short distance of Yor, or if a rule-consistent amplifier is explicitly established on the page; fail if clear thought fragments still arrive from roughly 200 meters away with no new mechanism.

[CH10_KILL_COUNT_CONTRADICTION] Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:225 match the casualties demonstrated in the action sequence, or if the action sequence is revised so five armed men are unmistakably killed; fail if the text still states or implies five deaths while the hallway guards remain alive enough to be merely unconscious.
[RED-024] Ch 10 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.
[RED-035] Ch 10 'She'-opener sentences are at or below 7%.

[LW04-004] When Chapters 9 and 10 are read back-to-back, the last pages of Chapter 10 should leave a distinct new consequence on the reader, not just a stronger paraphrase of Chapter 9's telepathy-is-theft conclusion.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:137 makes clear why the incriminating record is reachable tonight and requires at least one concrete obstacle, ambiguity, or inferential step before Yor can conclude the Garden is implicated; fail if she still opens a drawer and immediately receives a complete, legible conspiracy proof packet.
findings.problem: The chapter-defining reveal arrives as a plot handout. Yor opens the exact drawer Dominic reached for and finds a binder that cleanly exposes four years of Garden subcontracting, but the scene does not establish why this insurance file is on his desk tonight, why it is so legible to her so quickly, or what resistance makes the discovery feel earned instead of lucky.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the desk-search beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:125, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:137 so the records are present for a chapter-specific reason and Yor has to win the information. Viable approaches include making Dominic actively using the file for tonight's buyers, forcing Yor through a lock/code/mislabeled stack before she reaches the key page, or having her assemble the Garden link from multiple partial records rather than one self-explanatory binder.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:207 never enters Anya's interior and contains no telepathic-feed italics from Yor's mind; fail if the chapter still changes focalizer after Yor walks away or uses Anya's internal readout to explain the scene that has already landed.
findings.problem: After Yor's perimeter scene lands, the chapter hands off into full Anya interiority and telepathic transcript. That breaks the chapter's Yor-centered POV contract and turns Yor's destabilization into an explanatory coda instead of letting the Yor chapter end on Yor's own fracture and restraint.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the ending after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:207. Keep the chapter in Yor's close third through the exit, or render Anya's presence only from Yor's side before the chapter break. Do not move into Anya's interior at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:211, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:231, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:245, and do not use italicized telepathic reception to restate the chapter's meaning after Yor leaves.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the first telepathic fragment in the revised version occurs only after the text has placed Anya within a plausibly short distance of Yor, or if a rule-consistent amplifier is explicitly established on the page; fail if clear thought fragments still arrive from roughly 200 meters away with no new mechanism.
findings.problem: Anya starts receiving clear tactical thoughts from Yor 'two hundred meters out.' That blows past the established telepathy range and asks the reader to ignore a core world-rule exactly when the chapter depends on the power's limits to make the mother-daughter collision legible.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Revise the setup around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:221 so Anya only catches Yor's thoughts once they are physically within the established range, or add a specific in-scene mechanism that extends reception without contradicting the telepathy rules. If no such mechanism exists elsewhere in the book, the cleaner fix is to identify Yor visually first and delay mind-reading until close proximity.

Aggregator guidance:
- Cross-chapter context: Continuity sheet world rules: Anya's telepathy range is approximately 15-20 meters. Crowds can amplify noise but do not extend range. Anya cannot receive clear tactical thoughts from Yor at 200 meters. Either bring them within physical range first (visual identification, then telepathy at close proximity), or establish a specific in-scene mechanism that extends reception without contradicting the rules. The cleanest fix: Anya identifies Yor visually at distance, then reads her thoughts only after they are within ~20 meters.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:225 match the casualties demonstrated in the action sequence, or if the action sequence is revised so five armed men are unmistakably killed; fail if the text still states or implies five deaths while the hallway guards remain alive enough to be merely unconscious.
findings.problem: The chapter cannot keep straight how many people Yor actually kills. The action scene shows the cargo-van guard and Dominic die, while the two corridor guards are later described as still unconscious, yet both Yor's narration and Anya's coda summarize the operation as five kills. That is a direct factual contradiction inside the chapter, and it muddies the moral weight the ending wants to assign.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise either the action staging at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:93, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:151, or revise the aftermath language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:225, so the body count is internally consistent. If five deaths matter, put five unmistakable fatalities on the page. If they do not, change both summary moments to match the casualties actually shown.

findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 10 from 52 to ~10 (target ~8%).

findings.acceptance_test: When Chapters 9 and 10 are read back-to-back, the last pages of Chapter 10 should leave a distinct new consequence on the reader, not just a stronger paraphrase of Chapter 9's telepathy-is-theft conclusion.
findings.evidence: Chapter 9 ends with Anya defining intimacy with Damian as theft and contamination, then Chapter 10 closes by restating the same telepathy-as-violation thesis after Yor's mission, explicitly looping Damian back into the comparison. snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2433 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2451 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2706 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2710

evidence.excerpt: 2433: She kept kissing him. She kept her hands in his hair and her mouth on his and she did not pull away, because pulling away would be the correct operational response and she was not, in this moment, capable of the correct operational response. She was capable of nothing except the knowledge that she was stealing from him. Every second of this was a theft. His most private feelings, laid open to her not by his choice but by the fact of what she was — the thing she was born with, the ability she'd never asked for, the same ability that had let her betray Sylvia and deceive Loid and read her mother's buried doubts about the Garden. And now, here, this: the unguarded tenderness of a man who was kissing her because he believed they were meeting each other equally.

2451: That was the thing about telepathy that no one would ever understand, because no one knew. It didn't just give you information. It gave you feelings that weren't yours and made them indistinguishable from the ones that were. She had felt his loneliness, and now she felt lonely. She had felt his hope, and now she was hopeful. Was that connection? Or was it just contamination she'd mistaken for something real?

2706: Anya had received all of it. Standing behind the waste containers in the dark, close enough to hear her mother's boots grinding the gravel, she had taken in her mother's most private reckoning the way she took in everything: without permission, without announcement, without the possibility of being asked to stop.

2710: The violation wasn't romantic this time. It wasn't operational. It was the same violation it had always been, the one that didn't change shape depending on who she committed it against: structural, constant, built into the fact of her existence, committed against everyone she loved by a power she hadn't asked for and had never once been given consent to use. Damian's unguarded feelings in a safe house and Yor's private grief on a service road. The scale differed. The mechanism was identical.

findings.problem: Because the chapters are adjacent, the second climax lands less like escalation than like reprise. Chapter 10 has a major new family and conspiracy rupture available, but its final movement returns to the same generalized Anya-guilt register Chapter 9 has just exhausted.
findings.rewrite_direction: Let Chapter 10's close crystallize what is uniquely different about violating Yor's privacy after the Garden revelation: a changed mother-daughter leverage state, a concrete resolve, or a Yor-specific fear, rather than another universal statement of telepathy as violation.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
